meta_doc: Each template carries discoverability metadata so an authoring LLM (via the studio MCP list_templates tool) can pick the right template from a course-author intent WITHOUT fetching every config. Fields: when_to_use (intent triggers — when the LLM SHOULD reach for this), avoid_when (anti-patterns), media_profile (text-heavy|balanced|image-first|image-only|video|data|code|diagram), text_density (none|low|medium|high), layout (one-line shape), tags (free keywords for matching). Full slot schema stays in each templates/<id>/config.yaml and is returned by get_template.

templates.name: Title Slide
templates.when_to_use: Open a course, module, or major section. The very first slide; a big statement headline with optional subtitle.
templates.avoid_when: Mid-lesson content, or when you need imagery — use section-header for chapter breaks.
templates.layout: centered big headline + subtitle, no media

templates.name: Section Header
templates.when_to_use: Divider between chapters/modules. Big chapter number + section title; signals 'new part starts here'.
templates.avoid_when: The first slide of the whole course (use title) or for regular content.
templates.layout: huge chapter numeral left, title + description right

templates.name: Bullet List
templates.when_to_use: Enumerate 3-6 discrete points, takeaways, or features. The default 'list of things' slide.
templates.avoid_when: More than ~6 items (split slides), or when each item deserves an image (use image-grid/feature-cards).
templates.layout: title + vertical list of bullets

templates.name: Two Column
templates.when_to_use: Compare two related text blocks side by side, or split a topic into two parallel ideas.
templates.avoid_when: A true A-vs-B contrast with winner framing (use comparison) or image+text (use img-text).
templates.layout: two equal text columns

templates.name: Image and Text
templates.when_to_use: Explain ONE concept with a supporting image beside a short paragraph. The workhorse 'concept + visual' slide.
templates.avoid_when: You want the image to dominate (use big-image/full-bleed) or multiple images (use image-grid).
templates.layout: image pane one side, title+body the other

templates.name: Image Slide
templates.when_to_use: Show one image with a short caption. Screenshot, product shot, single illustrative photo.
templates.avoid_when: The image should bleed edge-to-edge (use full-bleed) or you need accompanying explanation (use img-text).
templates.layout: centered image + caption below

templates.name: Image Row
templates.when_to_use: Show 2-5 images side by side as equal peers — a set of examples, steps-as-photos, or a gallery strip.
templates.avoid_when: Images need captions/titles per cell as cards (use feature-cards) or a single hero (use big-image).
templates.layout: horizontal row of evenly-spaced images + optional title

templates.name: Quote
templates.when_to_use: Highlight a memorable quote, principle, or testimonial with attribution.
templates.avoid_when: A short emphasis statement without an author (use big-statement) or a definition (use callout).
templates.layout: large quotation + author/source

templates.name: Callout
templates.when_to_use: Draw attention to a key warning, tip, definition, or 'remember this' note with an icon/label.
templates.avoid_when: A full quote (use quote) or normal body content.
templates.layout: labeled box with icon + title + body

templates.name: Code Block
templates.when_to_use: Show a code snippet, command, or config with syntax styling — programming/technical lessons.
templates.avoid_when: Explaining code conceptually without showing it (use img-text/diagram).
templates.layout: title + monospace code panel

templates.name: Steps / Timeline
templates.when_to_use: Show an ordered process, sequence, or timeline of 3-5 steps.
templates.avoid_when: Unordered points (use bullet-list) or branching flows (use diagram).
templates.layout: numbered horizontal/vertical step sequence

templates.name: KPI Grid
templates.when_to_use: Show a grid of key numbers/metrics with labels and optional deltas.
templates.avoid_when: A single hero number (use stat-number) or a chart (use chart).
templates.layout: title + grid of metric cards

templates.name: Data Table
templates.when_to_use: Present structured tabular data — rows and columns of values.
templates.avoid_when: Comparison of 2-3 options (use comparison) or metrics (use kpi).
templates.layout: header row + data rows table

templates.name: Chart
templates.when_to_use: Visualize trends/proportions with a chart image or chart spec.
templates.avoid_when: Discrete numbers (use kpi) or raw data (use table).
templates.layout: title + chart area + optional caption

templates.name: Blank
templates.when_to_use: Escape hatch — a blank canvas when no template fits and you need raw HTML control.
templates.avoid_when: Almost always — prefer a real template so the deck stays consistent.
templates.layout: empty themed canvas

templates.name: Big Statement
templates.when_to_use: One short, punchy sentence that should land hard — a thesis, a provocation, a key idea. Minimalist: huge type, almost no other text.
templates.avoid_when: An attributed quote (use quote) or a list of ideas (use bullet-list).
templates.layout: single oversized centered statement

templates.name: Full-Bleed Image
templates.when_to_use: Edge-to-edge hero image with an overlaid title — high-impact section opener or mood-setting visual. Maximum image, minimum text.
templates.avoid_when: The image needs a caption beside text (use img-text) or must be uncropped (use image).
templates.layout: full-frame background image + overlaid title/eyebrow

templates.name: Big Image + Label
templates.when_to_use: A large dominant image with a small label/title strip — let the visual carry the slide. Image-first concept reveal.
templates.avoid_when: Image should fully bleed (use full-bleed) or needs a paragraph (use img-text).
templates.layout: large framed image + compact title bar

templates.name: Image Grid
templates.when_to_use: Show 4-6 images in a grid (2x2 / 3x2) — a mosaic of examples, a moodboard, or a set of outputs/screenshots.
templates.avoid_when: Few images in a line (use image-row) or images needing per-cell text (use feature-cards).
templates.layout: 2-3 column image grid + optional title

templates.name: Comparison (A vs B)
templates.when_to_use: Contrast two options head-to-head — before/after, old/new, this-vs-that — each with a label and bullet points.
templates.avoid_when: Two parallel non-competing ideas (use two-column) or 3+ options (use table).
templates.layout: two labeled panels with a center divider/VS

templates.name: Stat Number
templates.when_to_use: One giant hero number/percentage with a short label — a single statistic that should shock or anchor.
templates.avoid_when: Several metrics (use kpi) or a trend (use chart).
templates.layout: single oversized number + caption

templates.name: Feature Cards
templates.when_to_use: Show 3-4 features/concepts each as a card with an icon or image + short title + one line. Minimalist, image-forward feature overview.
templates.avoid_when: Plain text points (use bullet-list) or pure images (use image-grid).
templates.layout: row of 3-4 icon/image cards with title + caption

templates.name: Image + Quote
templates.when_to_use: A portrait/photo beside a quote with attribution — testimonial with a face, or a person + their words.
templates.avoid_when: Quote without an image (use quote) or a generic concept image (use img-text).
templates.layout: portrait image one side, quote + author other

templates.name: Title over Image
templates.when_to_use: Course/module opener that pairs a headline with a strong supporting image in a split layout — warmer than the text-only title.
templates.avoid_when: Pure text opener (use title) or full-bleed hero (use full-bleed).
templates.layout: headline panel + adjacent image panel

templates.name: Screenshot Frame
templates.when_to_use: Present a UI screenshot inside a browser/app window chrome (dots + address bar) so it reads as a real product surface. Use for product demos, dashboard reveals, feature tours, or any 'here's the actual screen' moment.
templates.avoid_when: A non-UI photo or an abstract image (use big-image); when you need callout annotations (use annotated-image).
templates.layout: browser-window frame (traffic-light dots + url bar) around a screenshot + caption

templates.name: Annotated Image
templates.when_to_use: Highlight specific areas of a screenshot, diagram, or photo with numbered callout pins. Ideal for product walkthroughs, UX critique, architecture diagrams, or any 'look here' moment where the image is primary and labels provide structured commentary.
templates.avoid_when: More than 4-5 pins (labels overflow the side rail), or when the image is decorative and needs no annotation.
templates.layout: image + numbered callout pins with a side label rail

templates.name: Image Caption Grid
templates.when_to_use: 2-4 images that each need their own caption — a feature comparison, a step-by-step visual sequence, a before/during/after series, or a portfolio grid. Per-cell context is the point.
templates.avoid_when: Captions are identical or unnecessary (use image-grid), or more than 4 images (captions become unreadable).
templates.layout: optional title + 2x2 grid, each cell image + caption below

templates.name: Before / After Image
templates.when_to_use: Contrast two states of the same subject — a redesign reveal, a refactor diff, a feature improvement. Side-by-side framing makes the delta obvious. Labels are customizable (Old/New, v1/v2, Problem/Solution).
templates.avoid_when: The two images aren't directly comparable, or the difference needs textual explanation rather than visual contrast.
templates.layout: two image panels with center divider + a label per side

templates.name: Polaroid Stack
templates.when_to_use: A playful, human-touch moment — team photo highlights, retro memories, event coverage, any 3-photo collage where a loose casual feel is intentional. Slight rotation signals storytelling over analysis.
templates.avoid_when: Formal data/technical decks where the playful rotation feels off-brand; more than 3 photos.
templates.layout: 3 rotated polaroid cards in a loose fan/stack with caption strips

templates.name: Logo Wall
templates.when_to_use: A collection of partner logos, tech-stack icons, or 'trusted by' marks. Each logo in its own bordered cell with an optional name. Credibility slides, technology overviews, integration ecosystems, sponsors.
templates.avoid_when: Logos have wildly different aspect ratios, or you need a single logo at large focal size (use big-image/split-media).
templates.layout: eyebrow + title + grid of bordered logo cells

templates.name: Video Poster
templates.when_to_use: Introduce or link a recorded demo, tutorial, or walkthrough — a strong poster image with a centered play-button overlay makes the video intent obvious without playing inline.
templates.avoid_when: A static screenshot with no video association (use big-image/screenshot-frame); primarily text content.
templates.layout: full-bleed poster image with centered play overlay + optional title/caption bar

templates.name: Split Media
templates.when_to_use: Two distinct images or video posters side-by-side (landscape) or stacked (portrait) with optional per-side labels — compare two products, a mobile vs desktop variant, or any pair where the media itself carries the story.
templates.avoid_when: Panels need heavy text annotation (use comparison); only one image (use big-image).
templates.layout: two equal media panels with thin divider + optional labels

templates.name: Code + Output
templates.when_to_use: Show a code snippet alongside its terminal result/stdout — CLI demos, API call + response pairs, script invocations, test run output. Left = mono code pane, right = terminal output box.
templates.avoid_when: Code with no output to show (use code-block); long prose explanations (use two-column).
templates.layout: horizontal split: code pane left, terminal output pane right (stacked in portrait)

templates.name: Definition
templates.when_to_use: Introduce a key term, concept, or piece of jargon — the dictionary-entry layout (eyebrow, giant term, accent rule, definition sentence) creates a pause that makes vocabulary stick. Great as a concept opener.
templates.avoid_when: Defining multiple terms at once (use bullet-list/two-column); concept needs visual media to explain.
templates.layout: centered: mono eyebrow, giant display term, accent rule, definition body

templates.name: 2x2 Matrix
templates.when_to_use: A classic quadrant / prioritization grid with two axes (effort vs impact, urgency vs importance). Each quadrant gets a label + optional note. Strategic planning, backlog prioritization, risk assessment.
templates.avoid_when: More than 4 cells (use table/process-flow); axes not naturally orthogonal.
templates.layout: 2x2 grid with X/Y axis labels + 4 labeled quadrant cells

templates.name: Timeline
templates.when_to_use: Show a sequence of dated milestones, product history, or roadmap phases. Horizontal connected dots (landscape) / vertical left-spine (portrait). Use when dates/order along a line matter.
templates.avoid_when: Cyclic or non-linear processes (use cycle); sequential steps without dates (use process-flow); quarter grids (use roadmap).
templates.layout: horizontal connected-dot rail / vertical left-spine

templates.name: Process Flow
templates.when_to_use: Linear, step-by-step processes where order matters — onboarding flows, deployment pipelines, sign-up funnels. Boxes connected by arrow glyphs, no diagram tool needed.
templates.avoid_when: Parallel or branching steps (use a flowchart tool); non-process ordered lists (use agenda/bullet-list).
templates.layout: left-to-right connected boxes with arrows / top-to-bottom in portrait

templates.name: Hierarchy Tree
templates.when_to_use: An org chart, reporting structure, component hierarchy, or category breakdown with one parent and 2-4 children. Brand-colored root on top, children below via crossbar connector.
templates.avoid_when: More than 2 levels of depth (gets cramped); lateral or many-to-many relationships.
templates.layout: root node centered top, children fanned below via trunk + crossbar

templates.name: Agenda
templates.when_to_use: Open a lesson, module, or presentation by setting expectations — numbered rows of topics, with optional time annotations doubling as a schedule.
templates.avoid_when: Unordered bullet lists (use bullet-list); items with completion state (use checklist).
templates.layout: header + rule + numbered list with optional right-aligned note column

templates.name: Checklist
templates.when_to_use: A list of items with explicit done/not-done state — launch checklists, pre-flight requirements, completion criteria. Done items show a green check, pending an empty box.
templates.avoid_when: All items same state or state is irrelevant (use bullet-list/agenda); complex multi-column data (use table).
templates.layout: vertical list with glyph column (done/pending) + text column

templates.name: Pros & Cons
templates.when_to_use: A balanced evaluation of a decision, technology, or approach — 'should we adopt X?', trade-offs of Y. Positive vs negative with check/cross semantic coloring (success green / danger red).
templates.avoid_when: Both sides are neutral options rather than advantages vs disadvantages (use comparison); more than ~5 items per side.
templates.layout: two columns: success-tinted pros left, danger-tinted cons right

templates.name: Roadmap (Phase Columns)
templates.when_to_use: A phased product/project roadmap with columns for time horizons — Now/Next/Later, Q1/Q2/Q3, Sprint/Backlog/Future. Each column holds a short list; the current column is brand-highlighted.
templates.avoid_when: Sequential steps in a single flow (use process-flow/steps); precise dates or Gantt timelines (use timeline).
templates.layout: horizontal phase columns, header + bullets, first column brand-accented

templates.name: Cycle / Loop Diagram
templates.when_to_use: A repeating circular process — Plan-Build-Ship-Learn, PDCA, an Agile sprint cycle, any feedback loop. The loop indicator + arrows make cyclicality explicit. Best with 3-5 stages.
templates.avoid_when: Strictly linear process with no feedback (use process-flow/steps); stages needing detailed sub-steps.
templates.layout: loop indicator above a row of circular nodes with arrows, first node brand-highlighted

templates.name: Chapter Image Divider
templates.when_to_use: A section divider that introduces a new chapter/module with visual impact — full-bleed background image with a dark scrim, a large chapter number, and the section title. The image-backed counterpart to section-header.
templates.avoid_when: No suitable image available (use section-header); regular content slides (this is transitional only).
templates.layout: full-bleed bg image with scrim; giant chapter number + title + subtitle anchored left

templates.name: Stat Grid
templates.when_to_use: Showcase 3-4 key metrics at once — 'Q2 at a glance', cohort numbers, a dashboard snapshot. Each cell: giant number + label + optional caption. Between stat-number (single) and kpi (6+ dense cards).
templates.avoid_when: More than 4 stats (use kpi); a single number should dominate the slide (use stat-number); non-numeric content.
templates.layout: row of large stat cells (2x2 in portrait), first cell brand-accented
